Title: User Registration with Valid Credentials
Test Data:
- Username: testuser
- Password: TestPass123!
- Email: test@example.com
Test Steps:
1. User fills in the registration form with valid credentials.
2. User submits the form.
3. Application sends a registration request to the server.
4. Server saves the user data in the database.
5. Server generates an OTP and saves it in the database.
6. Server sends the OTP to the user via email.
7. User receives the OTP and enters it into the application.
8. Server verifies the OTP against the saved OTP.
Expected Result:
- User is successfully registered.
- User receives an email with the OTP.
- OTP verification succeeds, and the user is redirected to the login page.

Title: User Registration with Invalid Email Format
Test Data:
- Username: testuser
- Password: TestPass123!
- Email: invalidemail
Test Steps:
1. User fills in the registration form with an invalid email format.
2. User submits the form.
3. Application attempts to send a registration request to the server.
Expected Result:
- Registration fails due to invalid email format.
- Error message is displayed indicating the email format is incorrect.

Title: Successful Login with Valid Credentials
Test Steps:
1. User fills in the login form with valid credentials.
2. User submits the form.
3. Application sends an authentication token to the server.
4. Server retrieves the user's password from the database.
5. Server verifies the password.
6. Server generates a token and saves it in the database.
7. Server sends the token back to the application.
8. Application opens the home screen.
Expected Result:
- User is successfully logged in.
- Home screen is displayed.

Title: Failed Login with Incorrect Password
Test Steps:
1. User fills in the login form with incorrect password.
2. User submits the form.
3. Application sends an authentication token to the server.
4. Server retrieves the user's password from the database.
5. Server verifies the password.
Expected Result:
- Login fails due to incorrect password.
- Error message is displayed indicating the password is incorrect.

Title: Request Password Reset with Valid Email
Test Steps:
1. User requests a password reset via email.
2. Server checks if the email is registered in the database.
3. Server sends a password reset link to the user's email.
4. User clicks the link and enters a new password.
5. Server communicates the password change to the user.
6. Server saves the new password in the database.
Expected Result:
- Password reset link is sent to the user's email.
- New password is successfully saved in the database.
- User receives confirmation of password change.

Title: Request Password Reset with Unregistered Email
Test Steps:
1. User requests a password reset via email.
2. Server checks if the email is registered in the database.
Expected Result:
- Email is not found in the database.
- Error message is displayed indicating the email is not registered.

Title: Load Initial Cards on Homepage
Test Steps:
1. User opens the homepage.
2. Application calls initCards(10) on the server.
3. Server requests 10 objects from the database.
4. Database sends 10 objects to the server.
5. Server fills the card with 10 objects and sends it to the application.
6. Application displays the filled cards to the user.
Expected Result:
- Homepage displays 10 initial cards.
- User can swipe through the cards.

Title: Load More Cards on Homepage
Preconditions: User is logged in and has viewed 10 cards
Test Steps:
1. User continues to swipe cards.
2. Application requests the next 5 objects from the server.
3. Server requests 5 objects from the database.
4. Database sends 5 objects to the server.
5. Server fills the card with 5 objects and sends it to the application.
6. Application displays the additional 5 cards to the user.
Expected Result:
- Homepage displays an additional 5 cards.
- User can continue to swipe through the cards.

Title: Matching Preferences Between Two Users
Test Steps:
1. User 1 swipes right on User 2's card.
2. Application sends trackObjectPref(true) to the server.
3. Server saves the preference in the database.
4. Server confirms the save to the application.
5. Server asks the application if there are any matches.
6. Server receives the response (checkMatch(false)).
7. User 2 swipes right on User 1's card.
8. Application sends trackObjectPref(true) to the server.
9. Server saves the preference in the database.
10. Server confirms the save to the application.
11. Server asks the application if there are any matches.
12. Server receives the response (checkMatch(true)).
13. Application displays the match screen to User 1.
Expected Result:
- User 1 and User 2 both express interest in each other.
- Match is confirmed and displayed to User 1.

Title: Send Message Between Two Users
Test Data:
- User 1: testuser1
- User 2: testuser2
- Message: Hello!
Test Steps:
1. User 1 types a message.
2. Application sends sendMessage() to the server.
3. Server saves the message in the database.
4. Server confirms the save to the application.
5. Server sends the message to User 2's application.
6. User 2's application retrieves the message from the database.
7. User 2's application displays a notification.
8. User 1 opens the chat.
9. User 1 views the messages.
Expected Result:
- Message is successfully sent from User 1 to User 2.
- User 2 receives a notification about the new message.
- User 1 can view the message in the chat.

Title: Send Empty Message Between Two Users
Test Steps:
1. User 1 types an empty message.
2. Application attempts to send sendMessage() to the server.
Expected Result:
- Sending an empty message is not allowed.
- Error message is displayed indicating the message cannot be empty.

Title: Send Push Notification After User Action
Test Steps:
1. User performs an action that triggers a push notification.
2. Apitandler processes the request.
3. Apitandler sends the notification to the Pushnotifications system.
4. Pushnotifications triggers the Realtimecommunication service.
5. Realtimecommunication sends the push notification to the user.
Expected Result:
- Push notification is successfully sent to the user.
- User receives the notification on their device.

Title: No Push Notification Sent When User Is Not Registered
Test Steps:
1. User performs an action that would normally trigger a push notification.
2. Apitandler processes the request.
3. Apitandler checks if the user is registered.
Expected Result:
- User is not registered.
- No push notification is sent.

Title: Add Job to Job Queue
Test Steps:
1. User creates a job.
2. Job is added to the job queue.
3. Job queue updates its current size.
Expected Result:
- Job is successfully added to the job queue.
- Job queue reflects the updated size.

Title: Remove Job from Job Queue
Preconditions: Job is in the job queue
Test Steps:
1. User removes a job from the job queue.
2. Job queue updates its current size.
Expected Result:
- Job is successfully removed from the job queue.
- Job queue reflects the updated size.

Title: Generate and Validate Token
Test Steps:
1. User generates a token.
2. Token is stored with an expiry time.
3. Token is validated using the validate method.
Expected Result:
- Token is successfully generated.
- Token validation returns true if within expiry time.

Title: Validate Expired Token
Test Data:
- User: testuser
- Token Expiry Time: 1 hour ago
Test Steps:
1. User generates a token.
2. Token expires after 1 hour.
3. Token is validated using the validate method.
Expected Result: - Token validation returns false due to expiration.

Title: Get and Set Cache Value
Test Data:
- Key: testkey
- Value: testvalue
- Expiry Time: 1 hour
Test Steps:
1. Cache sets a value with an expiry time.
2. Cache gets the value using the same key.
Expected Result:
- Value is successfully set in the cache.
- Value is retrieved correctly before expiry time.

Title: Retrieve Expired Cache Value
Test Data:
- Key: testkey
- Value: testvalue
- Expiry Time: 1 hour ago
Test Steps:
1. Cache sets a value with an expiry time.
2. Cache gets the value using the same key.
Expected Result:
- Value is not retrieved due to expiration.
- Cache returns null or an error indicating the value has expired.

Title: Log Error Report
Test Data:
- Timestamp: Current time
- Message: Error occurred
- Level: Critical
- Details: Detailed error information
Test Steps: 1. System logs an error report.
Expected Result: - Error report is successfully logged with all provided details.

Title: Log Error Report with Missing Details
Test Data:
- Timestamp: Current time
- Message: Error occurred
- Level: Critical
- Details: (missing)
Test Steps: 1. System logs an error report with missing details.
Expected Result:
- Error report is logged with available details.
- Missing details are noted as null or default values.
